cases.prompt: What does the Drupal paragraphs_browser module provide?
expected_points: A modal browser form widget for Paragraphs entity_reference_revisions fields
Editors pick a paragraph type from user-defined, filterable groups
Each paragraph type can show a preview image and description
Requires the paragraphs module

cases.prompt: Which config entity does paragraphs_browser define and what does it store?
expected_points: Config entity id paragraphs_browser_type (a Browser Type)
Stores ordered groups (label + weight) keyed by group machine name
Stores map of paragraph-type-id to group machine name
Config name pattern paragraphs_browser.paragraphs_browser_type.{id}
Ships a default 'content' browser type

cases.prompt: What field widgets does paragraphs_browser add and to which field type?
expected_points: paragraphs_browser (Paragraphs Browser EXPERIMENTAL)
entity_reference_paragraphs_browser (Paragraphs Browser Classic)
Both apply to entity_reference_revisions fields
A widget setting chooses which Browser Type to use

cases.prompt: How do you set up a Paragraphs Browser so editors see grouped paragraph types?
expected_points: Add a Browser at Structure > Paragraph Types > Manage Browsers
Add Groups to the browser
Assign paragraph types to groups via the Configure Groups tab on each paragraph type
On the field's Manage Form Display set the widget to a Paragraphs Browser widget and pick the browser

cases.prompt: What permission controls administering Paragraphs Browser browsers and groups?
expected_points: All admin routes require 'administer paragraphs types' (from the paragraphs module)
The module ships no permissions.yml
The entity's declared 'administer paragraphs browser' permission is never actually defined

cases.prompt: How are a paragraph type's browser preview image and description stored?
expected_points: As third-party settings on the paragraphs_type entity under provider 'paragraphs_browser'
Keys image_path and description
Added via a fieldset on the paragraph type edit form (hook_form_alter)
Falls back to the Paragraphs icon when image_path is unset
